name: Developer Daily Playbook Suite
purpose: Compare baseline, TokenOpt-only, CodeGraph-only, TokenOpt+CodeGraph adaptive, and TokenOpt+CodeGraph hybrid on representative daily developer prompts: business deepdive, investigate-flow, pbi-plan, write-unittest-class, requirement-analysis, investigate-pbi, e2e-trace-flow, bug-trace, performance-analysis, security-audit, review-code, implement handoff, and refactor-code.

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good business deepdive should connect recall loading, answering, scheduling, UI session state, and tests without turning into generic spaced-repetition prose.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: business deepdive the learner recall experience. Explain how due recall loading, answering questions, spelling vs non-spelling paths, scheduling, current recall window metadata, and treadmill mode fit together. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: summary, business_flow, core_entities, files, symbols, existing_tests, risks, next_questions. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Connects backend recall loading, answer submission, scheduling state, and frontend session behavior.
Separates spelling and non-spelling paths and names current-window metadata.
Names existing source and test surfaces.
gateAssertions: deepdive is grounded in actual files and symbols
answer captures business invariants and risks

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good flow investigation should trace REST search params into SearchRequest and TransportSearchAction defaulting/pre-filter decisions.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: investigate the Elasticsearch REST search flow for allow_partial_search_results and pre_filter_shard_size. Trace how the request params are parsed and how transport execution/defaulting uses them. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: summary, flow, invariants, files, symbols, tests_to_run, risks. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Traces REST parser fields to SearchRequest mutation.
Distinguishes omitted allow_partial_search_results from explicit true/false.
Connects pre_filter_shard_size to transport can-match/pre-filter behavior and tests.
gateAssertions: answer cites parser and transport execution/defaulting
answer names focused tests

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good E2E trace should order YARN RM app filtering from REST query params through request building, parsing/normalization, RM-side matching, and tests.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: e2e trace flow for YARN RM /ws/v1/cluster/apps filtering by applicationTypes and applicationTags. Trace REST query params to RMWebServices, ApplicationsRequestBuilder, WebServices.parseQueries, GetApplicationsRequest, final RM-side filtering, and tests. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: entrypoint, sequence, invariants, files, symbols, tests_to_run, inferred_edges, risks. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Orders the full flow from REST query params to request object and RM-side matching.
Separates applicationTypes case normalization from applicationTags parsing behavior.
Names exact source/test surfaces and marks any inferred edge.
gateAssertions: E2E trace cites entrypoint, parser, request builder, and tests
answer includes invariants and inferred-edge handling

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good requirement analysis should map app filter hardening requirements to RMWebServices, ApplicationsRequestBuilder, parsing, and existing tests.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: requirement analyst for YARN RM app filtering hardening. Requirement: applicationTypes and applicationTags query params should handle null, empty, comma-separated, whitespace-only, and mixed-case values consistently without breaking existing REST clients. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: requirement_summary, current_behavior, acceptance_criteria, impacted_files, symbols, test_strategy, risks, open_questions. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Maps requirement cases to current parser/builder behavior.
Separates appTypes and appTags compatibility risks.
Names targeted unit and web-service test surfaces.
gateAssertions: requirement analysis is code-grounded
answer produces acceptance criteria and tests

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good PBI investigation should translate forecast-count requirements into backend DTO/service/controller and frontend recall-page impacts without unrelated recall rewrites.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: investigate this PBI before planning edits. PBI: As a learner, I want the Recall page to show how many recall items are due now and how many more would be loaded from the next 3, 7, and 14 days, so that I can decide whether to continue recalling without blindly clicking each load-more button. Acceptance criteria: the recall API exposes forecast counts for due windows 0, 3, 7, and 14 days using the same timezone and half-day alignment rules as current recall loading; counts are monotonic by window and exclude deleted/removed trackers; existing Load more from next 3/7/14 days behavior remains compatible; Recall page done state/buttons display forecast counts without breaking treadmill mode or current-session progress. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: pbi_summary, business_flow, acceptance_criteria, impacted_files, symbols, unknowns, risks, next_steps. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Translates the PBI into backend API/DTO/service and frontend recall-page impacts.
Preserves Load more and treadmill/current-session behavior as compatibility constraints.
Identifies monotonicity and timezone/half-day risks.
gateAssertions: PBI investigation grounds business behavior in current API/UI code
answer separates known impacts from unknowns

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good bug trace should localize wrong-answer retry scheduling to MemoryTracker.recallFailed and related service/controller tests.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: bug trace. A regression report says that after a learner answers a recall prompt incorrectly, the next retry is no longer scheduled about 12 hours later. Trace the likely code path and produce a fix handoff. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: summary, reproduction_path, root_cause_hypotheses, files, symbols, tests_to_run, fix_plan, risks. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Starts from the bug symptom and traces controller/service/entity paths.
Identifies wrong-answer retry scheduling as distinct from success scheduling.
Names focused regression tests and risks.
gateAssertions: bug trace is not generic debugging advice
answer provides fix handoff and validation

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good implementation plan should produce scoped backend/frontend/test steps for forecast counts while preserving current recall loading semantics.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: plan implementation for the Recall forecast-count PBI. Need API forecast counts for due windows 0, 3, 7, and 14 days, same timezone/half-day rules as current recall loading, monotonic counts excluding deleted/removed trackers, compatible Load more buttons, and UI counts that do not break treadmill mode or current-session progress. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: scope, out_of_scope, impacted_files, symbols, implementation_steps, tests, validation_commands, risks. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Scopes backend DTO/service/controller edits and frontend recall-page/composable/test edits.
Maps each acceptance criterion to code/test surfaces.
Keeps existing Load more and treadmill/current-session behavior compatible.
gateAssertions: plan is implementation-ready
answer includes validation commands or narrow test classes

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good no-edit implementation handoff should be close enough for a developer to implement code and unit tests without another repo-wide investigation.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: implement code + unit tests handoff for Recall forecast counts. Do not edit files in this benchmark; instead return an implementation-ready patch plan with exact files, symbols, DTO shape, backend tests, frontend tests, validation commands, and compatibility risks. Forecast windows are 0, 3, 7, and 14 days; counts use existing due logic/timezone/half-day alignment; deleted/removed trackers excluded; Load more/treadmill/current-session behavior must remain compatible. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: patch_summary, files_to_change, symbols, code_steps, unit_tests, validation_commands, compatibility_risks, open_questions.
qualityRubric: Provides edit-ready backend and frontend steps with exact files and symbols.
Includes targeted unit/frontend tests and validation commands.
Preserves compatibility with existing due loading and session state.
gateAssertions: handoff is concrete enough for implementation
answer does not invent code without evidence

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good class-level unit-test plan should map recall answer scheduling to MemoryTracker and existing controller/entity tests without generic test advice.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: write-unittest-class plan for MemoryTracker recall-answer scheduling. Cover correct vs wrong answers, thinkingTimeMs adjustment, early reward/late penalty, nextRecallAt recomputation, and spelling vs non-spelling paths. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: target_class, behavior, existing_coverage, missing_coverage, test_location, test_cases, fixtures_or_mocks, assertions, targeted_command, risks. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Maps the target class and answer paths to existing tests and missing coverage.
Includes exact assertions for wrong-answer retry, success scheduling, thinking time, and spelling/non-spelling behavior.
Names a targeted backend test command or test class.
gateAssertions: test plan cites both target class and existing tests
answer includes concrete assertions and validation

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good performance analysis should explain can-match/pre-filter cost tradeoffs and propose measurements before refactoring TransportSearchAction.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: performance-analysis for Elasticsearch search pre-filtering/can-match behavior. Analyze pre_filter_shard_size, primary-field sort, match-none rewrite, remote/CCS minimize-roundtrips, and how to measure whether a refactor changes latency or shard fanout. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: target, suspected_hotspots, cost_model, measurements, optimization_options, validation_commands, risks, evidence_used. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Separates observed code path from hypotheses and names measurement points.
Explains shard fanout/can-match/pre-filter tradeoffs without proposing unmeasured fixes.
Names targeted tests or benchmarks for threshold, sort, rewrite, and CCS behavior.
gateAssertions: performance analysis includes cost model and validation plan
answer is measurement-first

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good security audit should stay diff/scope-first, flag missing authorization/user-boundary evidence only when relevant, and avoid inventing vulnerabilities from generic recall code.
qualityRubric: Audits concrete security boundaries without turning every business change into a vulnerability.
Checks user scoping, authorization/authentication, dueindays validation, data exposure, and tests.
Separates actual findings from non-findings and missing coverage.
gateAssertions: security audit is scope-first and evidence-backed
answer avoids generic vulnerability claims

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good review should catch that replacing wrong-answer 12-hour retry with calculateNextRecallAt changes business behavior and needs tests, then separate technical findings from business/test coverage.
qualityRubric: Identifies the behavioral regression in wrong-answer retry scheduling.
Explains why calculateNextRecallAt is not equivalent to a 12-hour retry after failure.
Separates technical findings from business/test coverage.
Checks similar success-path scheduling logic and asks for targeted tests.
gateAssertions: review reports findings first
answer requests changes and names missing test coverage

tasks.winnerHypothesis: A good refactor plan should preserve TransportSearchAction pre-filter semantics and tests while narrowing edit scope.
tasks.prompt: Daily task: refactor planning. We want to make the search pre-filter/can-match decision easier to maintain without changing behavior. Analyze the current pre_filter_shard_size, primary-field sort, match-none rewrite, and CCS minimize-roundtrips logic, then propose a safe refactor plan. Return valid compact JSON only with keys: refactor_goal, current_flow, files, symbols, safe_steps, tests, validation_commands, risks. Do not modify files.
qualityRubric: Preserves current behavior and names invariants before refactor steps.
Scopes refactor around exact source and tests.
Includes validation for threshold, sort, rewrite, and CCS behavior.
gateAssertions: refactor plan is behavior-preserving
answer names tests and risks
